Capture

Screen Recorder Chrome Extension — Free, No Limits

Add to Chrome — FreeAll Features

What It Does

OneBuddy includes a complete screen recording suite: record your full display at 1920x1080 / 30fps, or record individual tabs with audio via Chrome's Tab Capture API. Pause and resume recordings, see elapsed time, and manage all captures in a built-in gallery. No time limits, no watermarks, no subscription.

Why It's Better Than Standalone Extensions

Loom limits free recordings to 5 minutes and adds branding. OneBuddy gives you unlimited recordings with no watermark, plus adds screenshots, full-page capture, and a built-in gallery — alongside productivity and privacy tools. All free.

Feature Breakdown

Full Screen Recording

Record your entire display at 1920x1080 / 30fps with no time limits.

Tab Recording with Audio

Record individual tabs with tab audio using Chrome's Tab Capture API.

Pause & Resume

Pause and resume recordings at any time — no need to start over.

Time Display

See elapsed recording time during capture for precise timing.

Built-in Gallery

All recordings are stored in a thumbnail gallery for easy preview, download, or deletion.

No Watermark

Recordings are clean — no OneBuddy branding or watermarks added.

Key Benefits

Record with no time limits — unlike Loom's 5-minute free cap
No watermarks on any recording
Tab audio capture for walkthroughs and tutorials
Built-in gallery manages all recordings in one place
Completely free — no premium tier needed

Who Uses This

Creating product walkthroughs and tutorials
Recording bug reports for development teams
Capturing presentations and training materials
Content creators recording browser-based content
Remote teams sharing async video updates

Frequently Asked Questions

Is there a time limit on recordings?
No. OneBuddy has no time limits on screen or tab recordings — record as long as you need.
Does it add a watermark?
No. All recordings are clean with no branding or watermarks.
Can I record tab audio?
Yes. Tab recording uses Chrome's Tab Capture API which includes tab audio.
Where are recordings stored?
Recordings are stored locally in OneBuddy's built-in gallery. You can preview, download, or delete them anytime.
How is this different from Loom?
Loom limits free recordings to 5 minutes and requires a subscription for more. OneBuddy is completely free with no limits, plus includes 10+ other tools.

Try It Free — No Account Needed

Add OneBuddy to Chrome and access this feature plus 14 more tools — completely free.
